content.claim: Hungary has decade-plus unresolved legal accountability on discriminatory misclassification of Roma children: ECtHR *Horváth and Kiss v. Hungary* (2013) found ethnic discrimination in SNI/remedial-school placement; *Szolcsán v. Hungary* (2023) required national policy to end segregation in education; Committee of Ministers monitoring implementation reports unresolved case; European Commission 2016 infringement proceedings status remains opaque.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: Enforcement gap is documented but mechanisms for measuring progress toward eliminating discriminatory practices are not standardized
No quantitative baseline of discriminatory misclassification prevalence prior to judgment vs. post-judgment
Relationship between general SNI growth and Roma-specific misclassification trends not disentangled in available sources
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
